name: Wilhelmina
title: The Iron Monarch
investmentNote: Top 5 pull priority for physical FH/GR players. Both costumes required — skip if you don't plan to pull Iron Monarch.
lore: Wilhelmina is a regal, iron-willed queen of water — a character whose cold authority on the battlefield is matched only by the devastating precision of her strikes. Once a monarch of a fallen kingdom, she now channels her sovereignty into combat, each ability a declaration of absolute dominion over a single target. Her presence on any roster signals that the enemy's most dangerous foe will be shattered under an icy chain of relentless, compounding strikes.
overview: Wilhelmina is the premier single-target chainer in Brown Dust 2, capable of generating more chains on one enemy than anyone else in the game. Her kit revolves around her two core costumes — Iron Monarch and Water Park Queen — which work in tandem to create a loop of escalating chain counts that supercharge every physical attacker on the team. She is a fixture in Fiend Hunt and Guild Raid, appearing in roughly 70% of top compositions, and is one of the rare Combat Supports who also contributes serious damage in her own right.
keyInsight: Wilhelmina is classified as a "Combat Support" in Fiend Hunt tier lists — she belongs to both support and damage categories. This means she can fill a damage-dealer slot while still providing essential chain utility, making her exceptionally flexible and space-efficient in team building.

costumes.name: Iron Monarch
costumes.description: Wilhelmina's signature costume and the reason she's irreplaceable. Iron Monarch applies a massive chain-generation hit to a single target, stacking chains that boost every subsequent physical attack in the turn. At +4, the cooldown drops to 1 turn, meaning she can be used every single round — making chain maintenance effortless across the entire fight. The chain count generated scales with the number of prior chains on the target, creating an exponential feedback loop with Water Park Queen.

skills.name: Sovereign Chain
skills.desc: Generates a high number of chains on a single target. Chains scale chain bonuses for all physical attackers.

skills.name: Royal Presence (Passive)
skills.desc: Increases chain count generated based on current enemy chain stack. Synergizes with Water Park Queen bonus.

tips: Needs +4 to reach 1-turn cooldown — non-negotiable for competitive content.
Pair with Poolside Guardian Zenith who amplifies chain-based vulnerability.
Always use on the same target as your main damage dealer.

costumes.name: Water Park Queen
costumes.description: The amplifier to Iron Monarch's foundation. Water Park Queen increases the number of chains Wilhelmina generates per use, and the buff stacks additively with Pure White Blessing Refithea's chain bonus. The buff duration is short at lower upgrades — at +4, the duration extends enough to cover meaningful multi-turn windows. This costume transforms Wilhelmina from a great chainer into the single best chain generator in the game, period.

skills.name: Torrent Authority
skills.desc: Increases chain count generated by Wilhelmina's abilities. Stacks with external chain bonus buffs like Refithea.

skills.name: Queen's Decree
skills.desc: Buff duration increased at +4. Must be maintained alongside Iron Monarch rotation.

tips: Useless without Iron Monarch. Always bring both.
+4 is mandatory to maintain buff duration across turns.
Stacks with Pure White Blessing Refithea for maximum chains per turn.

costumes.name: Frozen Queen
costumes.description: Wilhelmina's accessible standard costume. Frozen Queen is a single-target specialist that provides solid water-element physical damage without chain utility. Useful in Last Night early on but falls off dramatically once Iron Monarch is obtained. Invest here only if you need water single-target coverage and lack her limited costumes.

skills.name: Glacial Judgment
skills.desc: Single-target water physical attack. No chain generation. Good damage modifier for early-game content.

tips: Free to obtain — solid for Last Night in early game.
Do not invest upgrade resources here if you plan to pull Iron Monarch.
Can serve as a filler on turns where Iron Monarch is on cooldown at low investment.

weapons.name: Sovereign's Edge
weapons.description: Best-in-slot for Wilhelmina in chain-focused compositions. The chain bonus directly amplifies the chains she generates, creating a multiplicative loop with her kit.

weapons.name: Frost Blade
weapons.description: Excellent when running Wilhelmina as a hybrid damage/chainer. The water DMG bonus adds meaningful personal damage in water-advantage fights.

weapons.name: Commander's Scepter
weapons.description: A reliable budget option. Lacks the chain-specific bonus of BiS weapons but is a solid all-rounder for players without specialized gear.

potentialUpgrades.label: Iron Monarch P3
potentialUpgrades.note: CD reduction — mandatory, enables 1-turn CD at +4

teams.name: FH Physical Core
teams.description: The gold standard Fiend Hunt physical team. Zenith's Poolside Guardian amplifies chain-based vulnerability, making Wilhelmina's chain generation directly increase Nebris's damage. Diana covers elemental advantage. This is the team you build toward for endgame FH.

rotation.action: Use Water Park Queen → Apply chain bonus buff
Use Iron Monarch every turn → Chains accumulate on target
Main DPS fires on target with stacked chains
Reapply Water Park Queen when buff expires

teams.name: Last Night Score Push
teams.description: Wilhelmina's chains create massive score multipliers in Last Night. Paired with Onsen Liberta and Shrine Maiden Granadair, the chain count can reach astronomical levels by the final turn, producing score-doubling outputs.

rotation.action: Use Water Park Queen + Shrine Maiden Granadair
Iron Monarch + Onsen Liberta every turn
Nebris fires with massive accumulated chains

teams.name: Guild Raid Comp
teams.description: In Guild Raids, Wilhelmina fills the single-target chainer role for Team 1 or 2. Celia handles multi-target debuffs while Wilhelmina piles chains on the primary target. Blade's counterattacks benefit enormously from the chain count accumulation.

rotation.action: Use Iron Monarch on primary target
Cycle Iron Monarch + Blade Apostle for compounding chain/counter damage

tips.title: Both Costumes or None
tips.body: Water Park Queen is useless without Iron Monarch. Never bring one without the other in endgame content — they function as a single unit.

tips.title: +4 is the Minimum
tips.body: Both core costumes need +4. At lower investments, Iron Monarch can't maintain 1-turn cooldown and Water Park Queen's buff won't last long enough to matter.

tips.title: Always Target the Same Enemy
tips.body: Chain counts accumulate on a single target. Make sure every ability — yours and your carry's — hits the same unit to maximize the chain bonus.

tips.title: Zenith is Her Best Partner
tips.body: Poolside Guardian Zenith converts chain counts into physical vulnerability, turning Wilhelmina's chains into a direct damage multiplier. This pair appears in 70%+ of top FH runs.

tips.title: Stack Refithea's Chain Bonus
tips.body: Water Park Queen's chain bonus stacks additively with Pure White Blessing Refithea's chain bonus. Running both maximizes total chains per turn — a must for Last Night score pushing.
